factors.summary: Catalysts include the reinstatement of the Federal R&D Tax Credit, which is expected to positively impact tax rates.

evidence.text: On December 18, 2015, the Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes Act was enacted which permanently reinstated the Federal Research and Development Tax Credit retroactive to January 1, 2015. This favorable tax credit had previously expired on December 31, 2014. We expect our effective income tax rate for 2016 to be in the range of 22 to 23 percent.

factors.summary: The company is managing shareholder returns through a combination of buybacks and dividend increases, which reflects a cautiously confident approach. However, there are potential impacts on shareholder returns due to an expected effective income tax rate in the range of 22 to 23 percent.

evidence.text: Cash dividend payments increased $6 million. During the six months ended March 31, 2016, $86 million of cash dividend payments were made to shareowners as compared to $80 million during the same period last year. The increase was primarily due to a 10 percent increase in our quarterly cash dividend paid on common stock to $0.33 per share, which was effective beginning with dividends paid in June 2015.
Our effective income tax rate for 2016 is expected to be in the range of 22 to 23 percent, which may impact shareholder returns.
